column1: The shift from an individual contributor's execution focus leads to a manager's {{c1::enabling}} focus.
A manager must shift from making tactical decisions to considering {{c1::systemic impact}}.
High-performing teams shift from a "blame" culture to a "{{c1::learning}}" culture.
column2: Important for building trust and open communication.

column1: In the SMART goal framework, 'A' stands for {{c1::Attainable}}.
In the SMART goal framework, 'R' stands for {{c1::Relevant}}.
column2: Meaning aligned with broader team and company goals.

column1: {{c1::Feedback}} is timely, actionable, and focuses on past/present behavior.
{{c1::Coaching}} is collaborative, inquisitive, and focuses on future growth.
In coaching, it's advised to avoid asking "{{c1::Why}}" because it can feel accusatory.
column2: Instead, use open-ended questions like "Tell me more about..."

column1: In the SBI feedback model, 'I' stands for {{c1::Impact}}, which is the result of the behavior.
column2: The other two are Situation and Behavior.

column1: Multitasking is discouraged because it can decrease productivity by up to {{c1::40%}}.
